d!d"d#g

dzd{d|g
print_function
division
absolute_import)
LookupOrder
ScriptList)
uint16
ScriptCountNNz
Number of ScriptRecords)
struct
ScriptRecordr	
z:Array of ScriptRecords -listed alphabetically by ScriptTagr
TagZ	ScriptTagNNz

ScriptNNz3Offset to Script table-from beginning of ScriptListr

LangSysNNz6Offset to LangSys table-from beginning of Script tabler
NNz5= NULL (reserved for an offset to a reordering table))
ReqFeatureIndexNNzVIndex of a feature required for this language system- if no required features = 0xFFFF)
FeatureCountNNzTNumber of FeatureIndex values for this language system-excludes the required feature)
FeatureIndexr
z8Array of indices into the FeatureList-in arbitrary order
FeatureList)
NNz&Number of FeatureRecords in this table)
FeatureRecordr
zkArray of FeatureRecords-zero-based (first feature has FeatureIndex = 0)-listed alphabetically by FeatureTagr

LookupListIndexr
z^Array of LookupList indices for this feature -zero-based (first lookup is LookupListIndex = 0)r

LookupList)

z<Array of offsets to SubTables-from beginning of Lookup table)
MarkFilteringSetNz
LookupFlag & 0x0010z
If set, indicates that the lookup table structure is followed by a MarkFilteringSet field. The layout engine skips over all mark glyphs not in the mark filtering set indicated.Z

STAT)
NNzGVersion of the table-initially set to 0x00010000, currently 0x00010002.)
DesignAxisRecordSizeNNz(Size in bytes of each design axis record)
DesignAxisCountNNz
Number of design axis records)
LOffsetTo(AxisRecordArray)Z
DesignAxisRecordNNzZOffset in bytes from the beginning of the STAT table to the start of the design axes array)
AxisValueCountNNz
Number of axis value tables)
LOffsetTo(AxisValueArray)
AxisValueArrayNNz`Offset in bytes from the beginning of the STAT table to the start of the axes value offset array)
ElidedFallbackNameIDNr6
z3NameID to use when all style attributes are elided.Z
AxisRecordArray)

Axis recordsr
AxisTagNNz.A tag identifying the axis of design variation)
AxisNameIDNNzWThe name ID for entries in the "name" table that provide a display string for this axis)
AxisOrderingNNz
A value that applications can use to determine primary sorting of face names, or for ordering of descriptors when composing family or face names)
uint8
	MoreBytesr
!Extra bytes.  Set to empty array.r
Z	AxisValuer

MATH)

MathConstantsNNzAOffset to MathConstants table - from the beginning of MATH table.)
MathGlyphInfoNNzAOffset to MathGlyphInfo table - from the beginning of MATH table.)
MathVariantsNNz@Offset to MathVariants table - from the beginning of MATH table.
MathValueRecord)
NNz!The X or Y value in design units.)
NNzuOffset to the device table - from the beginning of parent table. May be NULL. Suggested format for device table is 1.r
ScriptPercentScaleDownNNzDPercentage of scaling down for script level 1. Suggested value: 80%.)
ScriptScriptPercentScaleDownNNzSPercentage of scaling down for script level 2 (ScriptScript). Suggested value: 60%.)
DelimitedSubFormulaMinHeightNNz{Minimum height required for a delimited expression to be treated as a subformula. Suggested value: normal line height x1.5.)
DisplayOperatorMinHeightNNz`Minimum height of n-ary operators (such as integral and summation) for formulas in display mode.)
MathLeadingNNa?
White space to be left between math formulas to ensure proper line spacing. For example, for applications that treat line gap as a part of line ascender, formulas with ink  going above (os2.sTypoAscender + os2.sTypoLineGap - MathLeading) or with ink going below os2.sTypoDescender will result in increasing line height.)
AxisHeightNNz
Axis height of the font.)
AccentBaseHeightNNz
Maximum (ink) height of accent base that does not require raising the accents. Suggested: x-height of the font (os2.sxHeight) plus any possible overshots.)
FlattenedAccentBaseHeightNNz
Maximum (ink) height of accent base that does not require flattening the accents. Suggested: cap height of the font (os2.sCapHeight).)
SubscriptShiftDownNNz
The standard shift down applied to subscript elements. Positive for moving in the downward direction. Suggested: os2.ySubscriptYOffset.)
SubscriptTopMaxNNz
Maximum allowed height of the (ink) top of subscripts that does not require moving subscripts further down. Suggested: 4/5 x-height.)
SubscriptBaselineDropMinNNz
Minimum allowed drop of the baseline of subscripts relative to the (ink) bottom of the base. Checked for bases that are treated as a box or extended shape. Positive for subscript baseline dropped below the base bottom.)
SuperscriptShiftUpNNzVStandard shift up applied to superscript elements. Suggested: os2.ySuperscriptYOffset.)
SuperscriptShiftUpCrampedNNzFStandard shift of superscripts relative to the base, in cramped style.)
SuperscriptBottomMinNNz
Minimum allowed height of the (ink) bottom of superscripts that does not require moving subscripts further up. Suggested: 1/4 x-height.)
SuperscriptBaselineDropMaxNNz
Maximum allowed drop of the baseline of superscripts relative to the (ink) top of the base. Checked for bases that are treated as a box or extended shape. Positive for superscript baseline below the base top.)
SubSuperscriptGapMinNNz\Minimum gap between the superscript and subscript ink. Suggested: 4x default rule thickness.)
Z!SuperscriptBottomMaxWithSubscriptNNz
The maximum level to which the (ink) bottom of superscript can be pushed to increase the gap between superscript and subscript, before subscript starts being moved down. Suggested: 4/5 x-height.)
SpaceAfterScriptNNzfExtra white space to be added after each subscript and superscript. Suggested: 0.5pt for a 12 pt font.)
UpperLimitGapMinNNz`Minimum gap between the (ink) bottom of the upper limit, and the (ink) top of the base operator.)
UpperLimitBaselineRiseMinNNzTMinimum distance between baseline of upper limit and (ink) top of the base operator.)
LowerLimitGapMinNNzXMinimum gap between (ink) top of the lower limit, and (ink) bottom of the base operator.)
LowerLimitBaselineDropMinNNz[Minimum distance between baseline of the lower limit and (ink) bottom of the base operator.)
StackTopShiftUpNNz8Standard shift up applied to the top element of a stack.)
StackTopDisplayStyleShiftUpNNzIStandard shift up applied to the top element of a stack in display style.)
StackBottomShiftDownNNzlStandard shift down applied to the bottom element of a stack. Positive for moving in the downward direction.)
Z StackBottomDisplayStyleShiftDownNNz}Standard shift down applied to the bottom element of a stack in display style. Positive for moving in the downward direction.)
StackGapMinNNz
Minimum gap between (ink) bottom of the top element of a stack, and the (ink) top of the bottom element. Suggested: 3x default rule thickness.)
StackDisplayStyleGapMinNNz
Minimum gap between (ink) bottom of the top element of a stack, and the (ink) top of the bottom element in display style. Suggested: 7x default rule thickness.)
StretchStackTopShiftUpNNzBStandard shift up applied to the top element of the stretch stack.)
StretchStackBottomShiftDownNNzvStandard shift down applied to the bottom element of the stretch stack. Positive for moving in the downward direction.)
StretchStackGapAboveMinNNz|Minimum gap between the ink of the stretched element, and the (ink) bottom of the element above. Suggested: UpperLimitGapMin)
StretchStackGapBelowMinNNzzMinimum gap between the ink of the stretched element, and the (ink) top of the element below. Suggested: LowerLimitGapMin.)
FractionNumeratorShiftUpNNz+Standard shift up applied to the numerator.)
Z$FractionNumeratorDisplayStyleShiftUpNNzdStandard shift up applied to the numerator in display style. Suggested: StackTopDisplayStyleShiftUp.)
FractionDenominatorShiftDownNNz^Standard shift down applied to the denominator. Positive for moving in the downward direction.)
Z(FractionDenominatorDisplayStyleShiftDownNNz
Standard shift down applied to the denominator in display style. Positive for moving in the downward direction. Suggested: StackBottomDisplayStyleShiftDown.)
FractionNumeratorGapMinNNz
Minimum tolerated gap between the (ink) bottom of the numerator and the ink of the fraction bar. Suggested: default rule thickness)
FractionNumDisplayStyleGapMinNNz
Minimum tolerated gap between the (ink) bottom of the numerator and the ink of the fraction bar in display style. Suggested: 3x default rule thickness.)
FractionRuleThicknessNNzAThickness of the fraction bar. Suggested: default rule thickness.)
FractionDenominatorGapMinNNz
Minimum tolerated gap between the (ink) top of the denominator and the ink of the fraction bar. Suggested: default rule thickness)
FractionDenomDisplayStyleGapMinNNz
Minimum tolerated gap between the (ink) top of the denominator and the ink of the fraction bar in display style. Suggested: 3x default rule thickness.)
SkewedFractionHorizontalGapNNzMHorizontal distance between the top and bottom elements of a skewed fraction.)
SkewedFractionVerticalGapNNzVVertical distance between the ink of the top and bottom elements of a skewed fraction.)
OverbarVerticalGapNNz`Distance between the overbar and the (ink) top of he base. Suggested: 3x default rule thickness.)
OverbarRuleThicknessNNz8Thickness of overbar. Suggested: default rule thickness.)
OverbarExtraAscenderNNzPExtra white space reserved above the overbar. Suggested: default rule thickness.)
UnderbarVerticalGapNNz]Distance between underbar and (ink) bottom of the base. Suggested: 3x default rule thickness.)
UnderbarRuleThicknessNNz9Thickness of underbar. Suggested: default rule thickness.)
UnderbarExtraDescenderNNzbExtra white space reserved below the underbar. Always positive. Suggested: default rule thickness.)
RadicalVerticalGapNNzkSpace between the (ink) top of the expression and the bar over it. Suggested: 1 1/4 default rule thickness.)
RadicalDisplayStyleVerticalGapNNztSpace between the (ink) top of the expression and the bar over it. Suggested: default rule thickness + 1/4 x-height.)
RadicalRuleThicknessNNz
Thickness of the radical rule. This is the thickness of the rule in designed or constructed radical signs. Suggested: default rule thickness.)
RadicalExtraAscenderNNzNExtra white space reserved above the radical. Suggested: RadicalRuleThickness.)
RadicalKernBeforeDegreeNNz`Extra horizontal kern before the degree of a radical, if such is present. Suggested: 5/18 of em.)
RadicalKernAfterDegreeNNzXNegative kern after the degree of a radical, if such is present. Suggested: 10/18 of em.)
RadicalDegreeBottomRaisePercentNNz
Height of the bottom of the radical degree, if such is present, in proportion to the ascender of the radical sign. Suggested: 60%.r
MathItalicsCorrectionInfoNNzVOffset to MathItalicsCorrectionInfo table - from the beginning of MathGlyphInfo table.)
MathTopAccentAttachmentNNzTOffset to MathTopAccentAttachment table - from the beginning of MathGlyphInfo table.)
ExtendedShapeCoverageNNa9
Offset to coverage table for Extended Shape glyphs - from the  beginning of MathGlyphInfo table. When the left or right glyph of a box is an extended shape variant, the (ink) box (and not the default position defined by values in MathConstants table) should be used for vertical positioning purposes. May be NULL.)

MathKern)
HeightCountNNz2Number of heights on which the kern value changes.)
CorrectionHeightr#
zhArray of correction heights at which the kern value changes. Sorted by the height value in design units.)
Z	KernValuer#
Array of kern values corresponding to heights. First value is the kern value for all heights less or equal than the first height in this table.Last value is the value to be applied for all heights greater than the last height in this table. Negative values are interpreted as move glyphs closer to each other.r
MinConnectorOverlapNNzQMinimum overlap of connecting glyphs during glyph construction,  in design units.)

BaselineNNz
Baseline table.Z
BaselineFormat0)
NNz"Format of the baseline table, = 0.
DefaultBaselineNNzKDefault baseline value for all glyphs. This value can be from 0 through 31.
Delta
These are the FUnit distance deltas from the font
s natural baseline to the other baselines used in the font. A total of 32 deltas must be assigned.Z
BaselineFormat1)
NNz"Format of the baseline table, = 1.r/
AATLookup(uint16)Z
BaselineValuesNNz7Lookup table that maps glyphs to their baseline values.Z
BaselineFormat2)
StandardGlyphNNz
Glyph index of the glyph in this font to be used to set the baseline values. This glyph must contain a set of control points (whose numbers are contained in the following field) that determines baseline distances.
ControlPointr1
Array of 32 control point numbers, associated with the standard glyph. A value of 0xFFFF means there is no corresponding control point in the standard glyph.Z
BaselineFormat3)

Header.rb
FirstGlyphNNz2Glyph index of the first glyph in the class table.Z
prop)
NNuK
Version number of the AAT glyphs property table. Version 1.0 is the initial table version. Version 2.0, which is recognized by macOS 8.5 and later, adds support for the 
attaches on right
 bit. Version 3.0, which gets recognized by macOS X and iOS, adds support for the additional directional properties defined in Unicode 3.0.)
GlyphPropertiesNNz
Glyph properties.Z
GlyphPropertiesFormat0)
Format, = 0.)
DefaultPropertiesNNz
Default properties applied to a glyph. Since there is no lookup table in prop format 0, the default properties get applied to every glyph in the font.Z
GlyphPropertiesFormat1)
Format, = 1.)
NNzbDefault properties applied to a glyph if that glyph is not present in the Properties lookup table.)
PropertiesNNz5Lookup data associating glyphs with their properties.Z
opbd)
NNzPVersion number of the optical bounds table (0x00010000 for the initial version).)
OpticalBoundsNNz
Optical bounds table.Z
OpticalBoundsFormat0)
NNz(Format of the optical bounds table, = 0.)
AATLookup(OpticalBoundsDeltas)
OpticalBoundsDeltasNNzYLookup table associating glyphs with their optical bounds, given as deltas in font units.Z
OpticalBoundsFormat1)
NNz(Format of the optical bounds table, = 1.)
AATLookup(OpticalBoundsPoints)
OpticalBoundsPointsNNzaLookup table associating glyphs with their optical bounds, given as references to control points.re
LeftNNz+Delta value for the left-side optical edge.)
TopNNz*Delta value for the top-side optical edge.)
RightNNz,Delta value for the right-side optical edge.)
BottomNNz-Delta value for the bottom-side optical edge.rf
NNzQControl point index for the left-side optical edge, or -1 if this glyph has none.)
NNzPControl point index for the top-side optical edge, or -1 if this glyph has none.)
NNzRControl point index for the right-side optical edge, or -1 if this glyph has none.)
NNzSControl point index for the bottom-side optical edge, or -1 if this glyph has none.N)
__future__r
fontTools.misc.py23Z
otData
A/usr/lib/python3.9/site-packages/fontTools/ttLib/tables/otData.py
<module>
